steps.title: Set up the intersection equation
steps.content:
The graphs intersect where $x-c=-6(x-12)^2$. Expanding and moving everything to one side gives:
$$6x^2-143x+(864-c)=0$$

steps.title: Find the tangent boundary first
steps.content: Two distinct real solutions require the discriminant to be positive: $$143^2-4(6)(864-c)>0.$$ Simplify: $$20449-20736+24c>0$$ $$24c-287>0$$ $$c>\frac{287}{24}\approx11.96.$$

steps.title: Pick the listed value that gives two intersections
steps.content: $c=\frac{287}{24}$ gives one repeated solution, not two distinct solutions. The value must be greater than that boundary. Among the choices, only $24$ is greater, so choice D is correct.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — '7': $7 \lt 287/24 \approx 11.96$, so the discriminant is negative and there are no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice B — '11': $11 \lt 11.96$, so the discriminant is still negative. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{287}{24}$': Exactly at the threshold, the discriminant is $0$, giving one repeated solution instead of two distinct solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — '24': $24 \gt 11.96$, so the discriminant is positive and there are two distinct real solutions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
